sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this puzzle game

special_mechanics.name: Piece Spawning
special_mechanics.description: Pieces spawn from sides of screen and move toward center. Spawn rate increases per level. Two consecutive pieces never spawn from same quadrant.

physics_values.spawn_frequency: every second (more often in advanced levels)

special_mechanics.name: Proximity Bomb
special_mechanics.description: Special piece shaped like lightning bolt that clears pieces in 3x3 area centered at target

special_mechanics.name: Line Bomb
special_mechanics.description: Special piece (often gear-shaped) that clears entire row or column

tier_notes: Tier 3: Zoop is a real-time puzzle game, not a platformer or action game with traditional physics. No movement, gravity, or jump mechanics exist. Character is a static triangle in center of screen that controls piece interactions rather than moving through space. Physics parameters (walk speed, jump velocity, gravity, acceleration) are not applicable to this game's design. Documentation contains no technical physics data.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Zoop SNES article)
